assessment: The Payments tab gives a clean ledger with useful top-line totals (283, $4.97M received, $5,800 sent) and filter chips for Received/Sent. But the TYPE column is rendering raw HTML markup ('<span clas...') instead of a styled badge, which is an embarrassing P0 data-integrity-looking bug. The Reference column leaks internal IDs like 'No reference' and raw hashes where a method-specific label would read better, and the 4 unrecorded payments surfaced on the dashboard ('2 AR without matching invoices, 2 AP without matching bills') have no visible affordance here to triage them. The keyboard hint footer is a nice touch and the action split (Record Payment vs Void) is clear.
notable_strengths: Header totals ($4,973,788 received / $5,800 sent / 283 total) give immediate financial context before the table loads.
Keyboard affordance footer (Click/Shift+Click/Ctrl+Click/Ctrl+C/Escape) is discoverable and power-user friendly.
Received/Sent/All segmented filter is the right primary cut for a payment ledger.

findings.title: TYPE column renders raw HTML instead of a badge
findings.what_is_wrong: Every row in the TYPE column shows the truncated literal string '<span clas…' rather than a rendered Received/Sent status pill. The actual payment type (ACH, CASH, CHECK, etc.) is shown in the METHOD column, so TYPE is both broken and duplicative-looking.
findings.why_it_matters: A column full of visible HTML source in a finance ledger destroys trust in the data and makes the Received/Sent distinction impossible to read at a glance — users must rely solely on the filter chip or the amount alignment to tell direction.
findings.proposed_fix: Escape/render the status badge HTML properly so TYPE displays a colored pill ('Received' green, 'Sent' amber, 'Void' grey). If the column is meant to show direction, rename the header to 'DIRECTION' to disambiguate from METHOD.
findings.functionality_preserved: Touches only the cell renderer for the `paymentType` column documented in the baseline's filterable-table spec; filtering/sorting on `paymentType` is unchanged.

findings.title: No path from 'Unrecorded Payments' alert to this registry
findings.what_is_wrong: The dashboard flags '4 unrecorded payments · 2 AR without matching invoices · 2 AP without matching bills', but on the Payments tab there is no chip, filter, or banner to isolate those 4 rows among 283. The 'Sent $5,800' total in the header matches the AP figure but is not clickable.
findings.why_it_matters: Reconciliation is the whole reason an accountant lands here. Forcing them to eyeball 283 rows to find 4 orphans is the slowest possible path through a core daily workflow.
findings.proposed_fix: Make the '$4,973,788 received' and '$5,800 sent' totals clickable filters, and add a fourth chip 'Unmatched (4)' next to All/Received/Sent that filters to payments with no linked invoice/bill. Add a subtle amber row indicator for unmatched rows.
findings.functionality_preserved: Uses the existing `paymentType` filter plumbing and the `?invoiceId=`/`?orderId=` deep-link schema; no new router surface, just a preset filter state.

findings.title: Void action is ambiguous with no row selected
findings.what_is_wrong: The 'Void' button sits in the top-right next to 'Record Payment' with no selection state shown. It is unclear whether clicking it opens a void-by-ID dialog, voids the highlighted row, or is disabled until selection — and there is no selection checkbox column visible.
findings.why_it_matters: Void is a destructive, audit-trail-affecting action on a finance ledger. Ambiguity about its target is exactly the class of affordance that causes incident tickets.
findings.proposed_fix: Disable 'Void' until a row is selected, and when enabled, change its label to 'Void PAY-000060' (the selected payment #). Move 'Void' into the detail drawer as the primary destructive action rather than a top-bar button so it is always scoped to a specific payment.
findings.functionality_preserved: Void action and the row→drawer selection flow from the baseline ('Select a row to see details and take actions') are retained; only the button's enabled-state and label binding change.

redesign_sketch: Keep the 'Accounting' H1 and tab bar untouched. Under the tab strip, render a compact header row: 'Payments' title on the left; on the right, three clickable KPI tiles — Total (283) · Received ($4,973,788) · Sent ($5,800) — each acting as a filter preset. Below that, a single control bar: segmented [All · Received · Sent · Unmatched (4)] on the left, search on the right, and a meatball menu for column visibility. Move 'Record Payment' to a primary button top-right; remove the standalone 'Void' and make it a destructive action inside the row drawer. Table columns: PAYMENT # · DATE · DIRECTION (rendered badge, green/amber) · METHOD · AMOUNT (right-aligned, signed) · INVOICE/BILL · REFERENCE. Unmatched rows get a left-edge amber bar and a 'Link…' inline action. Keep the keyboard-hint footer pinned.
